source.name: Chapter 1.2: 1.4 Test Your Knowledge - Actions vs Responses
source.instructions: **How do actions differ from responses (utter_\*) in Rasa?**

answers.answer: Actions are defined in YAML; responses are defined in Python
Responses are used in flows; actions are only used in the domain file
Responses send predefined text only; actions run custom Python code and can do calculations, API calls, or dynamic logic
There is no difference; they are interchangeable

source.guidance: Responses (utter_*) are predefined text messages with no custom logic, defined in domain/basics.yml under responses. Actions (action_*) are custom Python code in the actions/ folder, registered under actions in the domain; they can execute any logic, access databases, perform calculations, and send messages via the dispatcher.
